items.title: Iran says U.S. denied visas to key WC officials
items.summary: Iran's soccer federation has accused the United States of "vindictive behavior" after saying that visas were refused for "key managerial and administrative members" of its World Cup team.

items.title: U.S. sharp in narrow WC sendoff loss to Germany
items.summary: The United States lost 2-1 to Germany at Soldier Field in Chicago on Saturday in its last match before the start of the FIFA World Cup, turning in another solid performance after Sunday's 3-2 win over Senegal.

items.title: Argentina's Balerdi out of WCup with calf injury
items.summary: Argentina defender Leonardo Balerdi has been ruled out of the World Cup due to a calf injury.

items.title: FIFA reverses WC water bottle ban after backlash
items.summary: FIFA has reversed its decision to bar World Cup fans from bringing refillable water bottles into the tournament's 16 stadiums across North America, following fierce backlash.

items.title: Messi 'doing well', could play World Cup warmups
items.summary: Argentina coach Lionel Scaloni said Lionel Messi is improving from muscle fatigue in his left hamstring and could play some minutes in the warmups before his team's World Cup debut on June 16.

items.title: SoFi workers authorize strike days before WCup
items.summary: Service workers at SoFi Stadium have voted to authorize a potential strike just days before the venue is scheduled to host the United States opening World Cup match against Paraguay on June 12.
